user_query:
Build a small Obsidian-style note bundle for my "Atlas Notes" project. Read these files under input/ and follow the requirements exactly:
- input/client_brief.md
- input/research_notes.md
- input/tasks.csv

Create three Markdown notes in output/notes/ with the exact filenames:
1) output/notes/Project Hub.md
2) output/notes/Design Decisions.md
3) output/notes/Research.md

Content and formatting requirements (Obsidian-flavored Markdown only):

A) output/notes/Research.md
- Add YAML frontmatter with: title: "Research"; tags: [research]; aliases: ["Research Notes"].
- Copy the sections "## Key Findings" and "## References" verbatim from input/research_notes.md (preserve headings and bullet items and their order).
- Include a LaTeX math example somewhere in the note: use inline math with the expression $O(n \log n)$.
- Include at least one Obsidian hidden comment block using %% on its own lines.

B) output/notes/Design Decisions.md
- Add YAML frontmatter with: title: "Design Decisions"; tags: [design]; aliases: ["Decisions"].
- Include this decision paragraph exactly once and append a block ID to the end of the same line: "Decision: Use PostgreSQL" followed by a space and ^decision-1 (e.g., "Decision: Use PostgreSQL ^decision-1").
- Add a wikilink back to [[Project Hub]] somewhere in the body.

C) output/notes/Project Hub.md
- Add YAML frontmatter with: title: "Project Hub"; tags: [project, active]; aliases: ["Atlas Notes Hub"]; cssclasses: [hub].
- Start with an H1 that includes the words "Atlas Notes".
- Add an inline tag #project in the body.
- Add an "info" callout that includes the exact phrase "Atlas Notes MVP goals" from the client brief (place this phrase inside the callout body). Also, highlight a short phrase using ==highlight== syntax somewhere in the note.
- Add a foldable callout of type "faq" that is collapsed by default (use the - variant in the callout marker).
- Add these internal links and embed:
  - A simple wikilink to [[Research]]
  - A simple wikilink to [[Design Decisions]]
  - A heading link to [[Research#Key Findings]]
  - An embed of the References section: ![[Research#References]]
  - A link to the specific decision block: [[Design Decisions#^decision-1]]
- Add a Mermaid diagram code block that shows a small graph connecting Project Hub to Research and Design Decisions (use ```mermaid fenced block and a graph statement).
- Add a footnote reference [^client] somewhere in the text, and define it at the bottom exactly as: "[^client]: Client brief 2026-06-30".
- Include at least one inline Obsidian hidden comment using %%like this%% in the text.
- Build a checklist from input/tasks.csv with one line per task using this mapping: status "todo" -> "- [ ] ", status "done" -> "- [x] ", status "blocked" -> "- [-] ". Each line must be formatted exactly as: "- [<box>] <title> (owner: <owner>)" using the title and owner from the CSV. Do this for every row in the CSV.

General rules:
- Use only Obsidian-flavored Markdown features for properties/frontmatter, wikilinks, callouts, embeds, block IDs, tags, comments, highlighting, math, Mermaid, and footnotes.
- Ensure all wikilinks and embeds point to the files you created under output/notes/ (e.g., [[Research]] refers to output/notes/Research.md). Do not link to input/ files.
- Preserve the exact bullet text under "## Key Findings" and "## References" from input/research_notes.md in output/notes/Research.md so that heading links and embeds work.
- Make sure the block ID ^decision-1 exists in Design Decisions and is referenced from Project Hub as [[Design Decisions#^decision-1]].
- Write only to the three files under output/notes/ specified above, and do not create additional files.
